title: Smoky German Cheddar Cheese Fries
description: A crowd-pleasing German-inspired snack featuring crispy fries smothered in a rich smoky cheddar cheese sauce, all made with air-frying for a healthier twist.
instructions: Cut the Russet potatoes into long, thin strips and rinse them under cold water to remove excess starch.
Dry the potato strips thoroughly with paper towels to remove excess moisture.
Heat 1 cup of vegetable oil in a large bowl to 350°F for frying.
Fry the potato strips in batches until they are crispy and golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per batch.
Drain the fried potatoes on paper towels and set them aside.
In a medium saucepan, melt 1/4 cup of butter over medium heat.
Whisk in 2 tablespoons of all-purpose flour to make a roux, cooking for 1 minute.
Slowly whisk in 1/2 cup of milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and stir in 1 cup of shredded cheddar cheese until it is melted and smooth.
Remove the sauce from heat and stir in 1 teaspoon of smoked paprika, 1/2 teaspoon of garlic powder, 1/2 teaspoon of salt, 1/4 teaspoon of black pepper, and 1/8 teaspoon of cayenne pepper.
Toss the fried potato strips in the smoky cheese sauce until they are well coated.
Transfer the smoky cheese fries to a serving plate and sprinkle with chopped parsley.
